list.subtitle_one: Across {{count}} connected provider · Q‑Agent learns each project before it tests
list.subtitle_other: Across {{count}} connected providers · Q‑Agent learns each project before it tests
list.title: Projects
list.emptyBody: Connect Azure DevOps, Jira or GitHub in Settings, then refresh to pull your projects in.

shared.title: Clone from shared
shared.subtitle: Ready-built projects maintained by admins — clone one instead of rebuilding knowledge.
shared.notReady: Not ready — no knowledge built yet

overview.calloutTitle: Project Knowledge powers every AI workflow
overview.calloutBody: Before analysing requirements, generating test cases, or writing Playwright, Q‑Agent reuses what it learned about this repository — architecture, page objects, fixtures and conventions.

knowledgeTab.emptyBody: A project can hold many repositories, each with its own knowledge base. Add the repos this project owns, then build knowledge per repo.

repoKnowledge.buildingBody: Cloning the repo and analysing the source — this can take a few minutes. You can leave this page; it keeps running.
repoKnowledge.notIndexedBody: Build this repository's knowledge base so Q‑Agent learns its routes, selectors, auth flow and conventions before generating tests.
repoKnowledge.needsRefresh: This repository changed since the last index. Rebuilding its knowledge base is recommended.

manualLogin.noLoginLocalAgent: No saved login yet — capture now and a browser opens on your Local Agent, or it opens automatically on the first run.
manualLogin.noLogin: No saved login yet — capture one now, or a browser will open on the first run.
manualLogin.capturingLocalAgent: A browser is opening on your Local Agent — log in there, then close the window to finish.
manualLogin.capturingHost: A browser opened on this machine — log in, then close the window to finish.

settingsForm.providerConnectionsDesc: Bind this project to a work-item source (where its tickets come from) and a repository source (where its code lives) — chosen independently. Manage connections in Settings.
settingsForm.applicationDesc: The default application URL the generated Playwright automation targets.
settingsForm.manualLoginDesc: Open a real browser on the host so an operator can log in before the run starts.
settingsForm.manualLoginNote: Before a run, a real browser opens on the machine running Q‑Agent so you can log in (e.g. Microsoft Entra); the session is reused until cleared.
settingsForm.testAccountsDesc: Credentials used by generated specs. Passwords are encrypted at rest and never shown — leave the password blank to keep the stored one.
settingsForm.environmentsDesc: Per-environment URLs. A run picks the environment matching its name (e.g. Staging).
settingsForm.name: Name
settingsForm.additionalSettingsDesc: Arbitrary project-specific key/values the automation generator can reference.

repos.bindProviderFirst: Bind a repository provider above first
repos.blurb1: Each repository gets its own knowledge base (built from the Project Knowledge tab). The
repos.blurb2: default repo is the one automation targets when a run doesn't specify one. Repos are cloned/pulled to
repos.blurb3: (private repos use the provider PAT), or use a local path if set.
repos.cloneUrlPlaceholder: clone URL (https://…) — used when no local path is set
